flashcards.front: What is recursion in C programming?
flashcards.back: Recursion is a programming technique where a function calls itself directly or indirectly to solve a problem by breaking it into smaller sub-problems until a base condition is reached.

flashcards.front: What is the base case in recursion?
flashcards.back: The base case is the terminating condition that stops the recursive calls. It is the smallest instance of the problem that can be solved directly without further recursion.

flashcards.front: What is the recursive case in recursion?
flashcards.back: The recursive case is where the function calls itself with modified parameters to solve a smaller version of the problem, moving closer to the base case.

flashcards.front: What causes stack overflow in recursion?
flashcards.back: Stack overflow occurs when there are too many recursive calls without a proper base case, exhausting the call stack memory. This happens when the recursion depth exceeds the system's stack limit.
Direct recursion occurs when a function calls itself directly within its own body.
Indirect recursion occurs when function A calls function B, which in turn calls function A, forming a cycle of calls.

Tail recursion is a special case where the recursive call is the last statement in the function. The compiler can optimize tail recursion by reusing the current stack frame instead of creating a new one.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between iteration and recursion?
flashcards.back: Iteration uses loops (for, while, do-while) to repeat code execution, while recursion uses function calls. Iteration is generally more memory efficient, while recursion provides cleaner solutions for inherently recursive problems like tree traversals.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of recursive factorial?
flashcards.back: The time complexity of recursive factorial is O(n) as it makes n recursive calls to compute n!.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of recursive factorial?
flashcards.back: The space complexity is O(n) due to the call stack depth, which stores n activation records during recursion.

flashcards.front: Why is the base case important in recursion?
flashcards.back: The base case prevents infinite recursion by providing a termination condition. Without it, the function would call itself forever until stack overflow occurs.

flashcards.front: What is the Fibonacci recursive time complexity?
flashcards.back: The naive Fibonacci recursive algorithm has exponential time complexity O(2^n) due to overlapping subproblems being recalculated multiple times.
Memoization is an optimization technique where computed results are stored in a cache to avoid redundant calculations. It converts exponential recursion to linear time complexity.

flashcards.front: Can all recursive problems be converted to iteration?
flashcards.back: Yes, theoretically any recursive algorithm can be converted to iteration using an explicit stack data structure, though the recursive version may be more readable and concise.
Head recursion is when the recursive call occurs at the beginning of the function before any other operations. The processing happens after returning from recursive calls.
